title: Eulophia peyrotii
taxonomy: Eulophia peyrotii is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Cymbidieae. The genus Eulophia is part of the subtribe Eulophiinae. Eulophia peyrotii is native to Madagascar, which may have contributed to its unique characteristics. Further classification may involve specific ranks such as species or infraspecific classification.

The plant is reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
